commit.message:
DM now owns the story: auto-generates secret story bible per campaign

- Added story_bible_generation_prompt() to prompts.py
  The DM invents: named world with a unique hook, a sympathetic villain
  with a ticking clock, 3-act structure (hook->rising stakes->climax),
  3 named NPCs with agendas, and 3 foreshadowing seeds to plant early.

- Added generate_story_bible() to DMNarrator
  Called once per campaign, cached in storage.story_bibles.
  Has fallback bible (Malachar the Undying / 1000-sacrifice ritual) if
  no API key is configured.

- Added 'Story Director Rules' block to dm_system_prompt()
  DM is now instructed to: plant seeds/foreshadowing proactively,
  advance the villain off-screen every few turns, give NPCs names and
  agendas, escalate toward Act 2, make consequences persist.
  Story bible is injected as a secret section the players never see.

- storage.py: added story_bibles dict + reset() cleanup

- game.py get_session_greeting: generates bible on first session,
  enriches greeting world context from bible when no explicit world set

- game.py _generate_dm_response + game_ws.py action handler:
  both now pass story_bible into narrate_exploration() so every DM
  response is informed by the campaign's narrative arc

* fix(bot): prevent bot from deadlocking when valid placements < SETTLEMENTS_PER_TURN

When a bot draws a terrain card but the board has fewer valid placements
than the required 3, placeSettlement never decrements remainingPlacements
to zero, so the phase never transitions to EndTurn and endTurn() silently
returns.  Add a safety net in triggerBotTurn: after all scheduled
placeSettlement calls complete, if the phase is still PlaceSettlements
force it to EndTurn before calling endTurn(), unblocking the game loop.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(bot+e2e): resolve bot game-over deadlock and E2E test failures

gameStore.ts:
- Import isBuildable to support board-full detection
- Add _consecutiveEmptyTurns module counter (reset in initGame)
- In triggerBotTurn: when bot has 0 valid placements, force phase to
  EndTurn and increment counter.  After all players skip a full round
  consecutively, set remainingSettlements = 0 to trigger game-over in
  endTurn() — prevents infinite loop when terrain cells are exhausted
- In endTurn: add boardFull condition as a secondary game-over trigger
- Change game-over check from === 0 to <= 0 for robustness

tests/e2e/game.spec.ts:
- game over test: bump test.setTimeout to 300s and waitForGameOver to
  240s; replace brittle scoreRows() (nth(6) locator) with direct CSS
  selector on the fixed overlay

playwright.config.ts:
- Set workers=1 globally; parallel workers caused bot-game setTimeout
  starvation leading to > 5-minute runtimes in CI

commit.message:
epic: End-to-End QA (#32)

* Initial commit from main

* feat: add Playwright E2E test suite and QA checklist

Implements end-to-end-qa-spec.md with full Playwright infrastructure
and 5 spec files covering all major features.

Test specs (37 total tests):
- setup-wizard.spec.ts: 5 wizard tests
- data-grid.spec.ts: 10 data grid tests
- toolbar.spec.ts: 8 toolbar tests
- navigation.spec.ts: 7 navigation tests
- error-states.spec.ts: 7 security/error tests

Infrastructure:
- playwright.config.ts with two projects
- global-setup.ts and global-teardown.ts for server lifecycle
- fixtures.ts with SeekiHelpers shared utilities
- seeki.toml.test and .env.test.example for test DB config
- Justfile targets: test-e2e and test-e2e-ui

Also adds QA-CHECKLIST.md with 183 manual checks across 9 sections.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(e2e): align test selectors with actual DOM structure

E2E tests were written against assumed selectors that didn't match the
real RevoGrid/Svelte DOM. Updated all spec files to use ARIA roles,
correct class prefixes (sk-grid-*), actual element IDs (#search-panel,
#conn-url, #ssh-auth), and light-DOM toolbar indicators. Added
mockSetupMode() to setup wizard tests so the wizard renders. Fixed ESM
__dirname in global setup/teardown.

* fix: numeric columns return null + strengthen SQL injection tests

Council-fix remediation for PR #32:

MUST_FIX:
- Fix numeric/DECIMAL columns returning null by using rust_decimal::Decimal
  instead of try_get::<String> which silently fails (postgres.rs + api/mod.rs)
- Strengthen SQL injection filter test to verify table still exists after
  injection attempt (not just "showing 0" which matches legitimate empties)
- Strengthen SQL injection search test to capture initial row count and assert
  injection doesn't return MORE rows (catches OR 1=1 success)

SHOULD_FIX:
- Back up existing seeki.toml before overwriting in globalSetup, restore in
  teardown (prevents user config loss on crash)
- Warn on empty env var expansion in test config (catches missing .env.test vars)
- Add seeki.toml.user-backup to .gitignore

* refactor(e2e): replace all waitForTimeout with proper Playwright waits

Eliminates all 27 hardcoded waitForTimeout calls across 4 spec files,
replacing them with deterministic wait strategies:

- Debounce waits (filter/search fill) → waitForResponse on /api/tables/*/rows
- UI panel appearance → expect(locator).toBeVisible() auto-retry
- Column count changes → waitForFunction checking DOM element count
- Sidebar animation → expect().toHaveClass() auto-retry
- localStorage writes → waitForFunction polling localStorage
- Search panel close → expect(panel).not.toBeVisible()

Adds SeekiHelpers.pendingRowsResponse() helper to fixtures.ts for
reuse across specs.

* chore: update council result for PR #32 and gitignore playwright-mcp

* fix(e2e): apply council findings — 6 test quality fixes

- Fix waitForGridLoaded to accept empty tables (was timing out on 0-row tables)
- Replace hardcoded 'vehicle_logs' in SQL injection test with dynamic table name
- Split pendingRowsResponse into ok-only (default) and any-status (error tests)
- Remove tautological >=0 assertions in filter/search tests
- Add pendingRowsResponse waits to sort cycling test for data verification
- Fix double release build in Justfile by passing SEEKI_SKIP_BUILD=1

* chore: add council result for PR #32 merge review

* fix(e2e): apply council session 2 findings — filter assertion + consistent beforeEach

- data-grid.spec.ts: per-column filter test now uses captured initialTotal
  (was captured but never compared — test was vacuously passing)
- error-states.spec.ts: SQL injection and edge case beforeEach blocks now
  use waitForAppReady + waitForGridLoaded (consistent with all other specs)
- Update council-result.json with session 2 verdict and MEC-Miki verification

* fix(e2e): resolve all council tracked gaps

- Sort test now asserts data order changes between asc/desc/unsorted
- Pagination test waits for status bar DOM update (fixes race condition)
- Setup wizard uses explicit page.unroute instead of LIFO ordering
- Multi-browser support: Firefox + WebKit opt-in via SEEKI_ALL_BROWSERS=1
- New Justfile target: test-e2e-all for cross-browser runs
- CI workflow: .github/workflows/e2e.yml with PostgreSQL service container
- Seed SQL: tests/fixtures/seed.sql mirrors MEC-Miki schema (200+80 rows)
- Replace null byte test input with realistic string (browsers strip \0)

* fix(e2e): apply council session 3 findings (MEC-Miki validation)

Session 3 council narrowly scoped to MEC-Miki validation rigor surfaced
two merge blockers and one Critical Discovery. All three addressed:

F1 (HIGH) — `global-setup.ts` now applies `tests/fixtures/seed.sql` via
`psql` after `waitForHealthy()`. Previously the seed was documentation
only; on a fresh checkout, NULL/boolean/numeric formatting tests and
two-table navigation could skip silently, making the suite non-reproducible.
`seed.sql` is now idempotent (TRUNCATE ... RESTART IDENTITY before inserts)
so repeated applies keep row counts stable at 200 + 80. Bypass with
SEEKI_SKIP_SEED=1.

F2 (MED) — `data-grid.spec.ts:131` filter assertion now has a lower
bound (`expect(filteredTotal).toBeGreaterThan(0)`) in addition to the
existing upper bound. Prevents a zero-match filter from silently passing.

CD-1 (Data Loss, informational) — `global-setup.ts:98-107` config backup
now only overwrites `seeki.toml.user-backup` if it doesn't exist yet, or
if the existing backup is already identical to `CONFIG_DST` (prior test
config). Prevents an interrupted Run #1 + Run #2 sequence from destroying
the user's real `seeki.toml`.

PR description amended to accurately characterize the suite as a generic
behavioral harness that exercises MEC-Miki runtime characteristics — not a
MEC-Miki schema-specific regression detector.

Recommendation: 2026-04-12-221500-council-pr32-mec-miki-session3.md

* fix(e2e): apply council session 4 findings

Session 4 audited d72decb and found two issues plus two minor follow-ups.

S4-1 (MED) — CD-1 backup-guard regression. d72decb compared backup===dst,
which silently destroys the user's config if they edit seeki.toml between
runs after a crashed teardown. The guard now compares dst!==configContent
(the expanded test config already in scope at global-setup.ts:86). Verified
across all 5 scenarios: first-run, interrupted-run, normal-2nd-run,
user-edits-between-runs, and orphaned-backup+edits.

S4-2 (MED) — Justfile test-e2e and test-e2e-all recipes gained a TRUNCATE
warning and documented the SEEKI_SKIP_SEED=1 bypass. Before d72decb the
suite was read-only; now it writes on every run, which deserves an explicit
warning on the documented invocation path.

FU-1 (low) — Credential guard at global-setup.ts now includes
SEEKI_TEST_DB_PASSWORD and lists the specific missing variable(s) in the
warning, replacing the imprecise generic message.

FU-2 (low) — Filter upper-bound tightened from toBeLessThanOrEqual to
toBeLessThan at data-grid.spec.ts:134. Seed data guarantees the filtered
set is a proper subset of the initial set, so the strict bound catches
broken filters that return the full unfiltered dataset.

Recommendation: 2026-04-12-223500-council-pr32-session4.md

* fix(e2e): emit console.warn when cell-formatting tests silently skip

Against arbitrary MEC-Miki data via SEEKI_SKIP_SEED=1, the NULL/boolean/numeric
cell-formatting tests at data-grid.spec.ts:283-326 would test.skip() without any
signal when the loaded table lacked the feature. Green runs could report zero
cell-formatting coverage. Adds console.warn before each test.skip() so skipped
coverage is visible in test output.

Found by: adversarial council session 5 (2026-04-13), CRITIC-1 Gap 2.

* fix(e2e): emit console.warn for remaining silent skip sites

Completes the silent-skip remediation started in 20daaae. Same pattern applied to:
- data-grid.spec.ts:151 (multi-filter AND test when <2 columns)
- data-grid.spec.ts:220 (pagination test when totalRows <= 50)

Both were flagged by council session 6 CRITIC-1 as same-class follow-ups.

Found by: adversarial council session 6 (2026-04-13), CRITIC-1 REBUTTAL.

Fix secondary IP detection in VLAN interface lookup (#97)

The relay was using IP address without prefix length (e.g. 192.169.0.1) to look up the VLAN_INTERFACE table in CONFIG_DB, but the actual keys include the prefix length (e.g. 192.169.0.1/22). This caused the secondary flag lookup to always fail, making the relay pick the last IP found on the interface instead of the primary one.

On topologies with multiple IPs on Vlan (e.g. t0-116 with both 192.168.0.1/21 and 192.169.0.1/22), the relay would use the secondary IP as giaddr/source, causing DHCP relay failures.

Fix: compute prefix length from netmask and include it in the CONFIG_DB lookup key, matching the key format used by CONFIG_DB (VLAN_INTERFACE|Vlan1000|192.169.0.1/22).

---------

Signed-off-by: Xichen Lin <lukelin0907@gmail.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

[Repo Assist] fix: resolve oneOf/anyOf with single $ref to referenced type (#375)

* fix: resolve oneOf/anyOf with single $ref to referenced type (#243)

When a schema uses `oneOf: [$ref]` or `anyOf: [$ref]` with a single entry,
resolve to the referenced type instead of falling through to `obj`.

This pattern is common in OpenAPI specs generated by tools (e.g. NSwag,
Kiota, AutoRest) and in OpenAPI 3.1 schemas that use oneOf/anyOf for
nullable type annotations.

Previously only `allOf: [$ref]` (the OpenAPI 3.0 canonical form) was
handled; the equivalent oneOf and anyOf forms produced `obj` instead of
the referenced type.

Changes:
- DefinitionCompiler.fs: add oneOf/anyOf single-ref handlers after the
  existing allOf single-ref handler; also extend the type-inference guard
  to check oneOf/anyOf single-entry type when schemaObj.Type is missing
- Schema.TypeMappingTests.fs: add 6 tests covering oneOf and anyOf with
  primitive and object aliases, required and optional variants

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* ci: trigger checks

commit.message:
Create complete Focus Android app — dopamine control system

Full Android project (Kotlin, Target SDK 34, Min SDK 26) implementing a
dopamine control system with the following features:

## Features
- Anti-Autopilot System: 10-second delay gate before blocked apps open
- App Blocking System: time-based locks, per-app daily open quotas
- Goal-Gated Unlock: complete Pomodoro sessions to earn opens
- Deep Work Engine: 25/5 Pomodoro timer with CountDownTimer
- Brutal Analytics: daily blocked count, focus time, life impact calc
- Streak System: daily streak tracking with XP rewards
- Replacement System: suggests Wikipedia, notes, breathing when resisting
- Emergency Mode: one-tap enable from dashboard
- Gamification: XP (+10/Pomodoro, +5/resist), levels (XP/100), titles

## Technical stack
- Kotlin, AndroidX, Material Design 3
- Room database (4 entities, 4 DAOs)
- Hilt dependency injection
- Navigation component with bottom nav (5 tabs)
- ViewBinding throughout
- AccessibilityService for app launch interception
- Foreground service for background monitoring
- WorkManager-ready architecture

## Project structure
- 33 Kotlin source files across data/ui/service/di/utils/receiver layers
- 9 layout XMLs + navigation graph + menu
- Vector drawable icons (nav + launcher + notification)
- GitHub Actions: debug build on PR, release APK + GitHub Release on v* tags

* Add nightly release workflow

Builds all 6 platform binaries from main daily at 04:00 UTC and
publishes them to a rolling 'nightly' GitHub prerelease. Also

- Version string: <pom-version>-nightly+<short-sha>

fix: use zellij action write for TUI sessions instead of FIFO

FIFO sends raw bytes to the PTY which bypasses zellij's input routing.
TUI apps like copilot need zellij action write-chars + write 13 to
properly submit input. This is the same path the dashboard WebSocket
uses (which works). FIFO still used for shell sessions.

feat: Add three-tier photo system with bcrypt and email invitations

- Implement family panel with wedding code + password authentication
- Add bcrypt password hashing (10 salt rounds) for all passwords
- Create email service with HTML invitation templates
- Enforce upload limits: 1 photo for guests, 10 for family
- Add photo deletion with role-based authorization
- Implement silent deletion with confirmation modal
- Auto-generate family passwords via super admin panel
- Update database schema with family password fields
- Install bcrypt and @types/bcrypt dependencies

Features:
- /family-panel/login: Family authentication
- /family-panel/gallery: View and delete any photo
- /family-panel/upload: Upload up to 10 photos
- /secret-coffee: Generate family passwords and send invitations
- All passwords hashed; never stored in plain text

Task 27: Knight armor sprite generation

- Add tools/generate_armor_sprites.py (Python/Pillow) that generates 4
  armored player sprite sheets from the base player_spritesheet.png:
  * Tier 2: Boots (metallic gray on lower legs/feet)
  * Tier 3: Boots + leg plates (greaves with plate lines and rivets)
  * Tier 4: Boots + legs + chestplate (torso plate mail, shoulder guards)
  * Tier 5: Full knight armor (helm with visor, gauntlets, gold accents)

- Add CMake custom command that regenerates armor sheets when the base
  spritesheet or generation script changes (requires Python 3 + Pillow)

- Update main.cpp: replace tint-only armor approach with actual sprite
  sheet switching via updatePlayerArmorSprites() helper. Re-registers
  all player animations with the correct armor-tier atlas path on
  level-up and on player setup (handles game load)

- Update architecture.md with new tools, assets, and sprite system docs
- Move completed Task 27 to CompletedTasks.md
